title: Pressure Control Solenoid “J” Performance/Stuck Off
possible_causes: Pressure Control Solenoid “J” Performance/Stuck Off circuit, commanded operation, or hydraulic pressure control fault.
Wiring, connector, power/ground, solenoid, TCM/PCM driver, valve body, ATF level/condition, or related DTC influence.
Hydraulic restriction, leakage, contamination, mechanical wear, or pressure regulation fault confirmed by manufacturer procedure.
check_order: Save DTCs, freeze-frame data, ATF temperature, gear/range state, pressure command/actual data, solenoid command state, and related DTCs.
Confirm vehicle-specific terminals, reference values, measurement conditions, solenoid identification, hydraulic port locations, and relearn requirements in the manufacturer service manual.
Inspect power/ground, control circuits, wiring, connectors, ATF level/condition, and commanded solenoid operation.
Separate circuit, solenoid, valve body, hydraulic, and mechanical causes using the manufacturer pressure and scan-tool procedure.
common_mistakes: Treating solenoid letter naming or pressure values as vehicle-universal.
Replacing a solenoid before checking ATF condition, valve body, and hydraulic leakage.
Running bidirectional or relearn procedures without the manufacturer prerequisites.
safety_notes: Use strong caution around hot transmission components, high hydraulic pressure, rotating parts, clutch/shift actuator movement, and driveline movement; secure the vehicle, allow cooling, relieve pressure, and follow the manufacturer procedure.
source: SAE J2012 / SAE J2012DA generic DTC definitions and Autel official DTC table based diagnostic guidance
